attacking_style: Attacks on short, steep gradients (13% cobbled climbs) or from 500m-2km out on punchy finishes, using sustained power rather than explosive acceleration. Rarely waits for final sprint when in form.
team_tactics: Relies on team tempo control to positioning, but shows repeated issues with team coordination (wrong breakaway timing, missing decisive splits). Works as protected leader but sometimes sacrifices personal result for team GC.
terrain_preference: Excels on cobbled climbs combining power and technical skill, punchy uphill finishes under 2km. Exposed on sustained climbs (Green Mountain, Cat 3s) and ultra-steep gradients like Mur de Huy where pure climbers dominate.
vulnerability: Sustained climbs cause visible difficulty; mistimes sprints when initiating too early; increasingly caught out by early race splits. Age-related decline in explosive efforts on key climbs evident from 2020 onwards.
key_patterns: Attacks 500m-2km from finish on punchy terrain, using sustained power to hold off chasers (3 instances)
Repeatedly caught behind decisive splits in classics, even as former winner/favorite (5 instances 2018-2021)
Strong positioning ability persists even in declining years, regularly in final groups without winning punch (4 instances)
Struggles on sustained climbs, explicitly in difficulty on anything beyond punchy gradients (4 instances)
Premature sprint initiation costs results despite exceptional power, leads out rivals (3 instances)
Tour de France yellow jersey defense 2018 showed tactical maturity, chasing time bonuses and managing gaps
form_trajectory: negative: Clear decline from 2020 onwards with passive racing, inability to sustain explosive efforts, and missing splits becoming pattern rather than exception
